data.title: Agibank launches new asset management division Agi Asset to expand market presence with credit expertise, a subsidiary of AGI Inc.
data.description: Agibank, a prominent financial institution blending digital banking efficiency with physical presence warmth, revealed the introduction of its latest business sector, Agibank Asset Management ("Agi Asset"), based in São Paulo. A subsidiary of AGI Inc. (NYSE: AGBK), Agibank aims to broaden its market reach through Agi Asset by utilizing its credit proficiency to develop investment opportunities. This launch signifies Agibank's strategic move toward diversification and market expansion, positioning itself as a key player in the financial industry.

data.title: OpenAI discontinues GPT-4o due to lawsuits, with only 0.1% of users still opting for the old AI among eight legal actions.
data.description: OpenAI has officially discontinued the use of GPT-4o and previous models in their ChatGPT system due to facing eight lawsuits. This decision comes as only 0.1% of users are still opting for the outdated AI.

data.title: Blue Hat postponed its 1-for-50 reverse stock split to March 9, 2026. Company had 154,912,000 shares and will trade under symbol "BHAT."
data.description: XIAMEN, China, Mar 04, 2026 - Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology ("Blue Hat" or the "Company") (NASDAQ: BHAT) has announced plans for a 1-for-50 reverse stock split on its ordinary shares, originally set for Mar 6, 2026. However, the implementation has been rescheduled to Mar 9, 2026. Before the split, as of Mar 3, 2026, the Company had 154,912,000 ordinary shares, par value $0.0000001 per share. Trading under the symbol "BHAT" on the Nasdaq Capital Market will continue after the split, with the new CUSIP number for ordinary shares post-split being G1329V 122.

data.title: Conifex Timber secures $19 million loan with BDC in Softwood Lumber Guarantee Program.
data.description: Today, Conifex Timber Inc. revealed that its subsidiary, Conifex Mackenzie Forest Products Inc., has finalized a $19 million secured term loan with the Business Development Bank of Canada as part of the Softwood Lumber Guarantee Program. The loan is aimed at supporting the company’s operations and growth.
